title: EMV Contactless e Tap to Mobile

sections.heading: Visão geral contactless
sections.bodyMd:
EMV contactless (tap) usa NFC para transações rápidas, geralmente abaixo de limites sem senha (CDCVM pode ser exigido acima do limite). O terminal e o cartão negociam capacidades via SELECT e GPO. O UX do app deve guiar o usuário a aproximar o cartão e mostrar feedback visual/sonoro claro.

TapToMobile allows merchants to accept contactless payments directly on near field communication (NFC)-enabled consumer and enterprise devices, without the need for an additional connected device, dongle or attachment.  The quality of the TapToMobile user experience is dependent on NFC read ranges, which determine how close and precisely positioned the payment device needs to be to the acceptance device for payment information to flow.

While traditional terminals have a high read range, consumer and enterprise devices – which were not designed with payments as their primary function – are more limited.  To address this challenge and promote a good user experience, EMVCo has defined minimum acceptance criteria and developed a supporting ‘reduced range’ approval process to evaluate the performance of consumer and enterprise devices for contactless payment acceptance.

*   While EMVCo uses the term TapToMobile, it can be used interchangeably with other industry terms such as softPOS, Tap on Phone, Tap on Mobile, Tap to Phone and Tap to Pay. 
*   EMV® Level 1 testing assesses compliance with the protocols defined within the EMV Chip and EMV Contactless Specifications, which covers the transfer of data between the terminal and the chip card or payment device (such as a smartphone or watch).  This includes tests to confirm how close the card or device and the reader need to be for reliable information to flow when a contactless payment is made.

**Pontos técnicos:**
- While EMVCo uses the term TapToMobile, it can be used interchangeably with other industry terms such as softPOS, Tap on Phone, Tap on Mobile, Tap to Phone and Tap to Pay.
- EMV® Level 1 testing assesses compliance with the protocols defined within the EMV Chip and EMV Contactless Specifications, which covers the transfer of data between the terminal and the chip card or payment device (such as a smartphone or watch). This includes tests to confirm how close the card or device and the reader need to be for reliable information to flow when a contactless payment is made.
- EMV® is a registered trademark in the U.S. and other countries and an unregistered trademark elsewhere. The EMV trademark is owned by EMVCo, LLC.
- Downloads and signs registration form.
- Submits the registration form to EMVCo.
sections.btgpayNote: Tap to Mobile e contactless exigem NFC estável, UX clara em caso de timeout ou anticolisão, e suporte a CDCVM quando o valor exigir verificação no dispositivo.

sections.heading: Tap to Mobile (SoftPOS)
sections.bodyMd:
Tap to Mobile transforma um smartphone ou tablet certificado em terminal de pagamento, sem hardware dedicado. Requer: dispositivo com NFC, SO suportado, ambiente seguro (TEE/StrongBox), app certificado e credenciamento do adquirente. No Flutter, a integração é via platform channels para o SDK nativo do provedor de pagamentos.

*   While EMVCo uses the term TapToMobile, it can be used interchangeably with other industry terms such as softPOS, Tap on Phone, Tap on Mobile, Tap to Phone and Tap to Pay. 
*   EMV® Level 1 testing assesses compliance with the protocols defined within the EMV Chip and EMV Contactless Specifications, which covers the transfer of data between the terminal and the chip card or payment device (such as a smartphone or watch).  This includes tests to confirm how close the card or device and the reader need to be for reliable information to flow when a contactless payment is made.

TapToMobile allows merchants to accept contactless payments directly on near field communication (NFC)-enabled consumer and enterprise devices, without the need for an additional connected device, dongle or attachment.  The quality of the TapToMobile user experience is dependent on NFC read ranges, which determine how close and precisely positioned the payment device needs to be to the acceptance device for payment information to flow.

“Advancing and evolving testing processes to reflect emerging industry developments is integral to promoting consistent, convenient and secure payments worldwide,” comments Aaron Armstrong, EMVCo Executive Committee Chair.  “The introduction of reduced range approval testing addresses the need to enhance the TapToMobile payment experience, enabling EMVCo and industry participants to start measuring the performance of TapToMobile devices and provide feedback on improving read range over time.

**Pontos técnicos:**
- While EMVCo uses the term TapToMobile, it can be used interchangeably with other industry terms such as softPOS, Tap on Phone, Tap on Mobile, Tap to Phone and Tap to Pay.
- EMV® Level 1 testing assesses compliance with the protocols defined within the EMV Chip and EMV Contactless Specifications, which covers the transfer of data between the terminal and the chip card or payment device (such as a smartphone or watch). This includes tests to confirm how close the card or device and the reader need to be for reliable information to flow when a contactless payment is made.
- EMV® is a registered trademark in the U.S. and other countries and an unregistered trademark elsewhere. The EMV trademark is owned by EMVCo, LLC.
- Downloads and signs registration form.
- Submits the registration form to EMVCo.

sections.heading: CDCVM
sections.bodyMd:
Consumer Device Cardholder Verification Method permite verificar o portador no próprio dispositivo (biometria, PIN do aparelho) em transações contactless de valor mais alto. O kernel decide quando CDCVM é necessário; o app deve solicitar autenticação local sem expor dados do cartão.

TapToMobile allows merchants to accept contactless payments directly on near field communication (NFC)-enabled consumer and enterprise devices, without the need for an additional connected device, dongle or attachment. To address this challenge and promote a good user experience, EMVCo has defined minimum acceptance criteria and developed a supporting ‘reduced range’ approval process to evaluate the performance of consumer and enterprise devices for contactless payment acceptance.
